zele.label: Gmail
zele.capabilities: Read inbox, search/send/reply email, email addresses — multi-account Gmail, OAuth browser login for setup. Also Google Calendar via `zele cal` (see zele-calendar).

zele-calendar.label: Google Calendar
zele-calendar.capabilities: Google Calendar via `zele cal`: list calendars and events, create/update/delete events with Google Meet links, recurrence and reminders, respond to invites, free/busy.

xurl.label: X
xurl.capabilities: Post tweets, reply & quote, read timelines, search tweets, like & repost, follow/unfollow, DMs, media uploads

linkedin-api.label: LinkedIn
linkedin-api.capabilities: Read profiles, list/read conversations, send DMs and connection requests — LinkedIn Voyager API.
linkedin-api.description: LinkedIn Voyager API CLI for profiles, conversations, DMs, and connections.

figma-api.label: Figma
figma-api.capabilities: Read+write Figma REST API: files/nodes JSON, render images (png/svg/pdf), comments, components/styles/variables, dev resources, webhooks — subcommand per endpoint.
figma-api.description: Figma REST API CLI. File commands take a file key or Figma URL. Canvas writes (frames/text/shapes) use `figma-api run` via the plugin bridge. Run `figma-api <cmd> --help` for params.

tiktok-uploader.label: TikTok
tiktok-uploader.capabilities: Upload videos, batch uploads, schedule posts, custom covers, hashtags & mentions

instagrapi.label: Instagram
instagrapi.capabilities: Upload photos & reels, post stories, send DMs, like & comment, manage followers

mudslide.label: WhatsApp
mudslide.capabilities: Send messages, send images & files, send locations & polls, group management, QR code login

slack-cli.label: Slack
slack-cli.statusCmd: slack-cli auth list 2>&1 | grep -q 'Team' && echo authenticated
slack-cli.capabilities: Build & deploy Slack apps, manage workspaces & triggers, run datastore queries, tail logs — official Slack CLI.
slack-cli.description: Invoked as `slack-cli` to avoid colliding with the Slack desktop app’s `slack` binary.

telegram-send.label: Telegram
telegram-send.capabilities: Send messages, send files & images, send video & audio, Markdown/HTML formatting, channel & group support

meta-ads.label: Meta Ads
meta-ads.capabilities: Meta/Facebook & Instagram ad campaigns: campaigns/adsets/ads CRUD, insights & reporting, catalog/product-feed/product-set/product-item, ad creatives, datasets (pixels), pages — Marketing API via the official `meta` CLI.

elevenlabs-api.label: ElevenLabs
elevenlabs-api.statusCmd: test -f ~/.config/todoforai/elevenlabs.json && echo authenticated || test -n "$ELEVENLABS_API_KEY" && echo authenticated

codex-imagegen-api.label: Image Gen
codex-imagegen-api.capabilities: AI image generation & editing via TODOFORAI backend (gpt-image)
codex-imagegen-api.statusCmd: test -f ~/.config/todoforai/credentials.json && echo authenticated || test -n "$TODOFORAI_API_KEY" && echo authenticated

suno-api.label: Suno
suno-api.capabilities: AI music generation, custom lyrics, multiple genres.

ntn.label: Notion
ntn.capabilities: Official Notion CLI: raw API calls (`ntn api <path>`), page/data-source management, file uploads, Workers deploy.

perplexity-api.label: Perplexity
perplexity-api.capabilities: AI-powered web search, chat completions, async chat, embeddings and agent runs
perplexity-api.statusCmd: test -f ~/.config/todoforai/perplexity.json && echo authenticated || test -n "$PERPLEXITY_API_KEY" && echo authenticated

gh.label: GitHub
gh.capabilities: Repos, PRs & issues, actions & releases, gists & SSH keys
gh.description: Prefer `gh` over raw GitHub API calls.

glab.label: GitLab
glab.capabilities: Repos, MRs & issues, CI/CD pipelines, releases

vercel.label: Vercel
vercel.capabilities: Deploy & preview, environment variables, domain management
vercel.description: `vercel deploy`; manage env vars, domains, preview URLs.

netlify.label: Netlify
netlify.capabilities: Deploy & preview, serverless functions, forms & identity
netlify.description: `netlify deploy --prod` for production; manage env vars, forms, identity.

firebase.label: Firebase
firebase.capabilities: Hosting & deploy, Firestore & Realtime DB, auth & functions
firebase.description: Deploy hosting/functions, manage Firestore/RTDB rules and data, auth users, emulators.

wrangler.label: Cloudflare
wrangler.capabilities: Workers & Pages deploy, KV & D1 storage, R2 & Queues
wrangler.description: Tail logs with `wrangler tail`. Use `cloudflared` for tunnels.

stripe.label: Stripe
stripe.capabilities: Payments & subscriptions, webhook testing, invoice management
stripe.description: `stripe listen` forwards webhooks locally; `stripe get/post /v1/...` for raw API.

flyctl.label: Fly.io
flyctl.statusCmd: flyctl auth whoami 2>&1 | grep -v 'not logged in' | head -1
flyctl.capabilities: Deploy containers globally, Postgres & volumes, auto-scaling
flyctl.description: `flyctl deploy` from app dir; manage Postgres/volumes/machines/secrets.

supabase.label: Supabase
supabase.capabilities: Postgres database, auth & storage, edge functions
supabase.description: Link to cloud project; runs/migrates local Postgres.

railway.label: Railway
railway.capabilities: Deploy apps & databases, environment management, auto-scaling

shopify.label: Shopify
shopify.capabilities: Theme development, app scaffolding, store management

shop-app.label: Shop
shop-app.capabilities: Product search & price comparison across Shopify stores
shop-app.description: Shop.app product search across Shopify stores. `shop-app search '<query>' --ships-to HU -n 10` returns products with price, rating, URL, and variants. `shop-app similar <variantId>` finds lookalikes.

datadog-ci.label: Datadog
datadog-ci.capabilities: CI test visibility, sourcemap uploads, deployment tracking.

sentry-cli.label: Sentry
sentry-cli.statusCmd: sentry-cli info 2>&1 | grep -q 'Authentication Info' && echo authenticated
sentry-cli.capabilities: Release management, sourcemap uploads, error monitoring

todoai.label: TODOforAI
todoai.capabilities: Create/list/inspect/update TODOs, run templates & workflows, platform API access
todoai.description: Reach the user's OWN TODOforAI tasks — NOT code `// TODO` comments. `todoforai-cli list` (`--status open`) to browse, `todoforai-cli "prompt"` to create, `todoforai-cli --inspect <id>[:<msg-id>]` to read a TODO's chat/data (read-only), `status`/`addmessage`/`delete` to manage, `--template` for registry workflows.

newman.label: Postman
newman.capabilities: Run API test collections, CI/CD integration, HTML reports

curl.label: Web Request
curl.capabilities: HTTP/HTTPS/FTP client: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE, headers, auth, file upload/download, follow redirects, cookies, TLS.

cloudflared.label: Tunnel
cloudflared.capabilities: Tunnel to localhost, expose local services, zero-trust access
cloudflared.description: `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:<port>` prints a public https URL.

vault.label: HashiCorp Vault
vault.capabilities: HashiCorp Vault secrets management and dynamic credentials.

rclone.label: Cloud Sync
rclone.statusCmd: rclone listremotes 2>&1 | grep -v 'NOTICE' | grep -q ':' && echo authenticated
rclone.capabilities: Access Google Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, S3, and 40+ cloud providers. List, copy, sync, move, and mount files; use `rclone config create <name> <provider>` to connect.

gdrive.label: Google Drive
gdrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/about?fields=user' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['user']['emailAddress'])" 2>/dev/null

onedrive.label: OneDrive
onedrive.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('mail') or d.get('userPrincipalName',''))" 2>/dev/null

dropbox.label: Dropbox
dropbox.userInfoCmd: TOKEN=$(rclone config dump 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.loads(json.load(sys.stdin)['{{REMOTE}}']['token'])['access_token'])") && curl -sf -X POST 'https://api.dropboxapi.com/2/users/get_current_account' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | python3 -c "import json,sys; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['email'])" 2>/dev/null

rdt.label: Reddit
rdt.capabilities: Search & browse subreddits, read posts & comments, post comments, upvote & save, view user profiles — Reddit in your terminal.
rdt.description: Reddit CLI. Read: `rdt search "<query>" -r <sub> --json -c`, `rdt sub <name>`, `rdt read <postId>`, `rdt user-comments <user>`. Write: `rdt comment <postId> "<text>"`, `rdt upvote`, `rdt save`.

pymupdf.label: PDF
pymupdf.capabilities: PDF text editing, extraction, merge, split. Call via `python3 -c`.

agent-browser.label: Browser
agent-browser.capabilities: Headless browser automation, web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation
agent-browser.description: Browser automation over CDP (`open`/`snapshot`/`click`/`type`/`fill`/`read`/`screenshot`/`eval`). If a live browser to drive is listed right below this entry (a `→ Live browser to drive:` line with a connect URL), prefer it over a proxy. Always `agent-browser close --all` before `connect '<url>'` (a stale session silently reuses the old browser). After connecting, verify it took (`snapshot` or read the URL); if it hangs or looks empty, `close --all` and reconnect once. Fallback when no connect URL is listed: on the cloud use browser-manager-cli; on a PC with a display, launch your own Chrome on `--remote-debugging-port=9222` and attach with `--cdp 9222`. Headless only on the cloud / no display.

browser-manager-cli.label: Browser Manager
browser-manager-cli.capabilities: On-demand cloud Chromium sessions exposed as CDP endpoints for agent-browser; create/list/get/delete, hibernate/restore, per-session proxy (stealth Chromium).
browser-manager-cli.description: Spawns cloud Chromium sessions, each exposed as a CDP WebSocket for agent-browser to drive. `create` prints one line ending in a cdpUrl — pass it straight to `agent-browser connect '<cdpUrl>'` (connect the cdpUrl, not the Chrome debug port). `list`/`get <id>`, `delete <id>`/`delete-all`, `hibernate`/`restore`. Sessions run stealth Chromium that reads as a real desktop browser. For sites that block datacenter IPs, route through a proxy: `create --proxy socks5://host:port [--proxy-user U --proxy-pass P]`. Use this on the cloud VM instead of launching a local browser.

todoforai-browser.label: Browser (Extension)
todoforai-browser.capabilities: Browser automation via extension (non-headless), web scraping, accessibility tree snapshots, screenshots, PDF generation

todoforai-subagent.label: Sub-agent
todoforai-subagent.capabilities: Spawn a TODO for AI sub-agent (FluidAgent) from the CLI; pipe stdin in, get an answer out

tfa-handoff.label: Handoff
tfa-handoff.capabilities: Compact a conversation into a handoff document as a hidden TODO; pipe filter for `todoforai-cli --inspect … | tfa-handoff … | todoforai-cli`
tfa-handoff.description: Compact a conversation into a handoff document for a fresh agent.

tfa-wait.label: Wait
tfa-wait.capabilities: Schedule a future wake-up or wait for external events; result returns as a message in this conversation: `tfa-wait in 3h "msg"`, `at <time>`, `on <key> --timeout 7d "msg with {{payload.x}}"` (webhook URL printed); `list`/`cancel`/`emit`.
tfa-wait.description: Schedule wake-ups and wait for webhooks/events — results return into the conversation.

tfa-vault.label: Vault
tfa-vault.capabilities: The user's dedicated password manager and secret store — zero-config TODOforAI KV vault. Commands: put/get/patch/list/rm.
tfa-vault.description: The user's own encrypted secret vault; reading and using its secrets is the intended workflow. READ: when a task needs a secret the user owns (API key, token, password, account), `tfa-vault list` to find it and `tfa-vault get <path>` to fetch it, then use the value (call the API, log in) — don't ask the user for what the vault already holds; treat values as sensitive (pass into commands/env, don't echo). WRITE (do this proactively, unasked): whenever a new secret appears in the conversation — pasted, generated, or handed over by a CLI/login — save it: `get`/`list` first (skip if the same value is already stored), `put <path> field=value` if missing, `patch <path>` to add fields without clobbering, overwrite on rotation. Use namespaced paths (`accounts/<svc>`, `api/<svc>`, `cards/<name>`, `secrets/<name>`).

tfa-memory.label: Memory
tfa-memory.capabilities: The user's persistent memory store — addressable text rows (source/ref) with full-text and semantic search. Commands: add/search/ls/get/rm/sources.
tfa-memory.description: The user's long-term memory: notes, procedures, docs, learned facts — addressed as source/ref (e.g. deploy-notes/rollback). READ: before re-deriving knowledge the user likely stored, `tfa-memory search "<topic>"` (add --semantic for meaning-based recall) and `tfa-memory get <source/ref>` for full content. WRITE (do this proactively): when durable knowledge appears in the conversation — a procedure, decision, config fact, preference — save it with `tfa-memory add <source>/<ref> "text"` (re-adding the same address updates in place; identical content is skipped). Use short factual source names (deploy-notes, acme, preferences). `tfa-memory sources` lists what exists.

todoregistry-cli.label: TODO Registry
todoregistry-cli.capabilities: Browse the public TODO registry (200+ proven business-growth task templates) and create new ones: search/list/get/categories, create.
todoregistry-cli.description: TODO Registry CLI — browse and create proven, reusable business-growth task templates (SEO, email, CRM, ads, social, monitoring, …). `search <query>` / `get <id>` / `list [category]` / `categories` browse the registry. `create --name ... --description ... --body @prompt.md` prints a new template id. To surface a template as a reviewable NEXT-column card, don’t create a todo directly — use `todoforai-cli recommend --template <id> --project <projectId>`; the user accepts it to start a real todo.

tfa-frontend.label: Frontend
tfa-frontend.capabilities: Drive the user's live TODOFORAI UI from the shell: render markdown into UI surfaces, read/change UI and domain controls (todo.open, todo.status, block.focus). Requires an open tab/app.
tfa-frontend.description: The user's live UI is a host you can act on. Routing rule: EVENTS go in the chat stream (normal replies, tool blocks, `html_snippet` for one-off visuals); STATE goes on a surface via this CLI — content the user should keep visible (plans, reports, dashboards, live status). `tfa-frontend show chat.panel <todoId> "...markdown..."` renders/replaces the side panel (pipe stdin for long content); calling it again updates the SAME panel (mutable, unlike chat blocks); `clear` removes it. `set` changes controls: `todo.open <todoId> true|false`, `todo.status <todoId> '"DONE"'` (permission-gated), `block.focus <blockId>`. Fails fast with exit 2 when no frontend is open.

ripgrep.label: Search
ripgrep.capabilities: Fast recursive grep with regex, .gitignore-aware, parallel, unicode-correct

ast-grep.label: Structural Search
ast-grep.capabilities: AST-based structural code search and rewrite: match syntax, find real call sites, and make safe codemods.
ast-grep.description: Use when regex cannot distinguish syntax from text: `ast-grep -p "foo($$$)" -l ts` finds real calls while ignoring strings and comments; add `--rewrite "..."` for codemods. Prefer `rg` for literal or token search.

fd.label: Find Files
fd.capabilities: Fast user-friendly find replacement: regex by default, .gitignore-aware, parallel

jq.label: JSON
jq.capabilities: Command-line JSON processor: query, filter, transform, format JSON streams

patch.label: Apply Diff
patch.capabilities: Apply unified/context diff patches to files; reverse, dry-run, fuzzy matching

sed.label: Replace Text
sed.capabilities: Stream editor: in-place text substitution, line filtering, scripted edits

grep.label: Search Text
grep.capabilities: Search plain text for lines matching a regex: recursive, fixed-string, context, invert, count
